settings.text: Thirty minutes with a senior engineer. We'll ask about your seat count, your stack, and what's breaking — then tell you whether we're a fit.

settings.title: Three ways to get in touch.
settings.description: Assessments and new-client inquiries come through the main line or email. Existing clients have a direct emergency number after hours.

settings.title: New clients & assessments
settings.text: Call or email to book a free thirty-minute assessment. A senior engineer — not a salesperson — will walk through your environment with you.

settings.title: Office
settings.text:
142 Fairlane Avenue, Suite 300

Columbus, OH 43215

Remote nationwide, on-site in Ohio & Kentucky

settings.title: A few things people usually ask.

settings.question: What happens on an assessment call?
settings.answer: Thirty minutes. We ask about seat count, current stack, and the top three things that are breaking or worrying you. You walk away with a written summary and, if it's useful, a tier recommendation. No obligation.

settings.question: What does onboarding look like if we sign?
settings.answer: Two to three weeks. We document your environment, deploy monitoring and EDR agents, set up the helpdesk channel, and run a cutover session with your team. The previous MSP keeps running things until we've fully taken over.

settings.question: Do you work with our internal IT lead?
settings.answer: Yes. Co-managed is a common setup — your lead keeps strategic control and project work; we take helpdesk overflow, after-hours, and the 24/7 monitoring stack. Roles are written into the engagement letter.

settings.question: We're a current client with an urgent issue — what's the fastest path?
settings.answer: Business hours: call the main line or open a ticket via the client portal. After hours: the emergency number routes to the on-call engineer with remote access to your environment.

settings.title: Prefer email?
settings.text: Send a note to hello@example.com with seat count and the top thing that's broken. We'll reply the same business day with a time for an assessment call.
